context: Tests whether the agent implements a clean ERP adapter interface with SAP-specific patterns (CSRF token fetching, padded order item numbers), correct cents-to-dollars currency conversion, a separate data transformation layer, and proper handling of base vs promotional prices.

checklist.name: ERPAdapter interface
checklist.description: Defines an abstract ERPAdapter interface (or abstract class) with distinct methods for orders, inventory, customers, and products — not a single generic 'call ERP' method

checklist.name: Separate mapper/transformer
checklist.description: Implements a dedicated mapping or transformation function/class to convert e-commerce order format to ERP format, separate from the adapter's HTTP call logic

checklist.name: SAP CSRF token fetch
checklist.description: Fetches the SAP CSRF token by first making a HEAD/GET request with 'X-CSRF-Token: Fetch' header before submitting mutating requests

checklist.name: CSRF token on mutating requests
checklist.description: Includes the retrieved CSRF token value in the 'X-CSRF-Token' header of POST/PUT requests to SAP

checklist.name: SAP order item numbering
checklist.description: Numbers SAP sales order line items starting at 10 and incrementing by 10 (000010, 000020, ...) zero-padded to 6 digits

checklist.name: Cents to dollars conversion
checklist.description: Divides monetary values (unit price, tax, shipping, discounts) by 100 when converting from e-commerce (cents) to ERP (dollars/base currency) format

checklist.name: Base price vs promotional price separation
checklist.description: Keeps ERP base prices and e-commerce promotional/discount prices separate; does NOT overwrite the base price with a discounted price during sync

checklist.name: Rate limiting in adapter
checklist.description: Implements or references rate limiting (token bucket, sliding window, or delay between batched calls) within the ERP adapter to avoid API throttling

checklist.name: External reference for idempotency
checklist.description: Uses the e-commerce order number (or another external reference) as a duplicate-check field when creating orders in the ERP

checklist.name: SAP OData API path
checklist.description: Uses the SAP OData API path /sap/opu/odata/sap for SAP requests

checklist.name: ERP-specific code isolated
checklist.description: SAP-specific field names (SalesOrderType, SoldToParty, etc.) appear only inside the SAP adapter implementation, not in shared e-commerce business logic
